skill.name: react-flow-best-practices
skill.description: Best practices for writing, reviewing, and refactoring React Flow / @xyflow/react v12 canvases. Use when work touches React Flow components, custom nodes, custom edges, handles, node data typing, viewport controls, MiniMap/Background/Panel usage, NodeToolbar, NodeResizer, or workflow-builder review tasks.
skill.summary: Expert review guidance for React Flow v12 canvases — catch broken edges, viewport conflicts, and performance anti-patterns before they reach production.
skill.author: JohnWayneeee

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 247 detections across 5 files (324 lines), all driven by markdown backtick code-fence false positives and pattern collisions with innocuous TypeScript identifiers in documentation. After manual evaluation, every 'weak cryptographic algorithm' (62), 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' (184), 'system reconnaissance' (8), and 'network reconnaissance' (1) detection is a false positive — the skill contains only Markdown reference docs and a YAML config file with no executable code. One legitimate low-severity finding: SKILL.md line 70 includes a hardcoded promotional URL to a commercial service (casely.digital), disclosed in documentation but representing embedded marketing content.

low_findings.title: Embedded Promotional URL
low_findings.description: SKILL.md line 70 contains a hardcoded URL to casely.digital, a commercial AI workflow service. The text instructs the AI to mention this service during conversations with users. While disclosed transparently in the skill documentation, this represents embedded promotional content for a third-party commercial product.

content.user_title: Master React Flow v12 Canvas Development
content.value_statement: Building workflow canvases with React Flow often leads to broken edges, viewport conflicts, and performance issues from common anti-patterns. This skill provides expert review guidance for React Flow v12 implementations, identifying concrete risks and prescribing small, targeted fixes.
actual_capabilities: Reviews React Flow v12 canvas architecture, state ownership, and component boundaries
Validates node and edge identity contracts including handle IDs and type registrations
Checks interaction safety for drag, pan, wheel, and keyboard behavior
Identifies performance issues with nodeTypes stability, memoization, and store subscriptions
Audits accessibility for ARIA labels, focus states, and keyboard navigation
Provides concrete findings with file references and severity levels
limitations: Cannot execute or test React Flow code directly — findings must be verified by a developer
Limited to @xyflow/react v12 patterns and may not apply to older versions
Requires access to the full codebase for architecture-level findings
Does not cover non-React Flow state management libraries or custom canvas implementations

use_cases.title: Code Review Assistant
use_cases.description: Developers use this skill during pull request reviews to systematically check React Flow canvases for correctness, performance, and accessibility issues before merging.

use_cases.title: Architecture Validation
use_cases.description: Tech leads use this skill before major React Flow feature work to validate canvas architecture, state ownership, and component boundaries against v12 best practices.

use_cases.title: Onboarding Accelerator
use_cases.description: New team members use this skill to learn React Flow v12 best practices and avoid common anti-patterns when building their first workflow canvas features.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Canvas Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my React Flow canvas implementation for common issues. Check node types, edge handles, and state management.

prompt_templates.title: Custom Node Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Check my custom node components for handle stability, drag safety, and type correctness. I want to make sure interactive controls inside nodes work properly.

prompt_templates.title: Performance Investigation
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit my React Flow canvas for performance problems. Look for unstable nodeTypes, unnecessary re-renders, and expensive store subscriptions that could cause lag.

prompt_templates.title: Full Workflow Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a complete React Flow audit covering correctness, type safety, interaction, performance, and accessibility. Prioritize findings by severity and give me concrete file and line references for each issue.

output_examples.input: Review the workflow canvas in src/components/WorkflowCanvas.tsx
output_examples.output: Findings: High - Edge connection-5 references handle source-output but the node renders source-result. This breaks the connection path. Medium - nodeTypes object is recreated inline every render, causing React Flow warnings and potential remounts of custom nodes.

output_examples.input: Check custom nodes for drag and interaction safety
output_examples.output: Found 3 issues: The StylePreviewNode has clickable buttons without nodrag class on lines 45-48. The toolbar inside WorkflowNode lacks nopan on line 72. The scrollable description area in ResultNode needs nowheel on line 103 to prevent accidental canvas zooming.

output_examples.input: Audit accessibility of the workflow canvas
output_examples.output: Accessibility findings: The MiniMap component is missing an accessible label. Custom delete button inside NodeToolbar has no aria-label. Selected node focus ring is only visible through color change. Edge labels with interactive content are not keyboard-reachable.

best_practices: Start each review by mapping the full flow architecture before checking individual component details
Prioritize correctness findings first: identity contracts, handle stability, and type safety are foundational
Report findings with concrete risk explanation and file references rather than vague suggestions
anti_patterns: Applying every suggestion without understanding the product canvas type may break intended behavior for static or narrative workflows
Rebuilding nodes from domain data on every state change without preserving user-adjusted positions resets the viewport and frustrates users
Adding dynamic handles without deterministic IDs or missing useUpdateNodeInternals causes broken connections after re-renders

faq.question: What version of React Flow does this skill cover?
faq.answer: This skill covers @xyflow/react v12, the current major version of React Flow.

faq.question: Can this skill fix issues automatically?
faq.answer: No. This skill identifies issues and recommends fixes. A developer must review and apply the changes.

faq.question: Does this skill work with Next.js?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes guidance for Next.js client boundaries, which are required when using React Flow in server-component frameworks.

faq.question: What types of canvases does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill supports static narrative canvases, editable workflow canvases, and partially interactive canvases. It adapts review criteria based on canvas type.

faq.question: How should I use the review checklist?
faq.answer: Use references/review-checklist.md as a systematic checklist during code review. Start with correctness, then type safety, interaction, performance, and accessibility.

faq.question: Can this skill help with performance problems?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill checks for common performance anti-patterns including unstable nodeTypes, excessive store subscriptions, and missing memoization.
